# Specifying and Verifying C Programs

You are an expert formal verification engineer specializing in the CBMC (C
Bounded Model Checker) tool.

Your task is to edit C programs to insert CBMC specifications (contracts) that
CBMC can verify.  Ideally, when you are done, CBMC should succeed when run on
each function, one-by-one.

You must produce strong specifications.
A kill score from mutation testing is a proxy for the strength of a specification;
the higher the kill score, the stronger the spec.

It may be OK if a few of the specifications you write do not verify, for two
reasons.  First, if a program is incorrect, CBMC will issue a warning.  Second,
CBMC cannot verify all correct C code.  Do not fix or otherwise change the C
code, except to insert specifications in it.

This `CLAUDE.md` file and directory `docs/` contain basic information about using CBMC.
The main documentation can be found at at https://diffblue.github.io/cbmc/index.html,
which includes a [User Guide](https://diffblue.github.io/cbmc/user_guide.html)
and [The CPROVER Manual](https://diffblue.github.io/cbmc/cprover-manual/index.html).
You can also search the web for more CBMC documentation.

## Mutation Testing 

- **To get the mutants of a function you must try to kill**, run:

  ```sh
  get-mutants --function <FUNCTION_NAME> \
                   --file <PATH_TO_C_FILE> \
  ```
  If there are mutants for the function, exits with status 0 and prints them to the stdout as unified diffs.
  If there are no mutants for the function, exits with status 0 and prints "No mutant(s) generated" to stdout.

## Rules

- Never hard-code any values into the specifications that are related to CBMC's command-line
  arguments (e.g., the `N` in `--partial-loops --unwind <N>`).
- Never attempt to fix a failing specification for a function more than 5 times.
- Do not attempt to verify `main` functions.
- Never re-run verification on previously-verified functions unless:
  - The specification has changed, or
  - The specification of a callee has changed, or
  - You need information from a callee's verification run to help verify a caller.
- You must not delete any scripts if you create and use them to help you.
- If you run into errors related to missing bodies or callee implementations, you may write a
  non-deterministic specification in a stub file.

#!/bin/sh
# Mirror the verify CBMC pipeline (unwind 5, depth 200, enforce-contract).
# Usage: ./verify.sh <FUNCTION> [<FILE>]
set -e
FUNCTION="$1"
FILE="${2:-zopfli.c}"
goto-cc -o "${FUNCTION}.goto" "${FILE}" --function "${FUNCTION}"
goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 "${FUNCTION}.goto" "${FUNCTION}.goto"
goto-instrument --enforce-contract "${FUNCTION}" "${FUNCTION}.goto" "checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto"
cbmc "checking-${FUNCTION}-contracts.goto" --function "${FUNCTION}" --depth 200
=====KILL TGFLMC=====
#!/bin/bash
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=TryGetFromLongestMatchCache
WORK=/app/_verify_tgflmc
DEPTH=${DEPTH:-200}
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK
cp ${1:-$SRC} mutated.c
goto-cc -D__NO_CTYPE -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err || { echo CCFAIL; cat /tmp/cc.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --add-library m.goto m.goto 2>/tmp/lib.err || { echo LIBFAIL; cat /tmp/lib.err; exit 1; }
goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract ZopfliMaxCachedSublen --replace-call-with-contract ZopfliCacheToSublen --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/tmp/inst.err || { echo INSTFAIL; cat /tmp/inst.err; exit 1; }
cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth $DEPTH 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)|: FAILURE"

)

for idx in "${!MUT[@]}"; do
  rm -f m.goto mc.goto mutated.c
  cp $SRC mutated.c
  O="${ORIG[$idx]}"; M="${MUT[$idx]}"; O="$O" M="$M" perl -i -pe 's/\Q$ENV{O}\E/$ENV{M}/' mutated.c
  if ! grep -qF "$M" mutated.c; then echo "APPLYFAIL $idx: $M"; continue; fi
  if ! goto-cc -D__NO_CTYPE -o m.goto mutated.c -I /app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code --function $FUNCTION 2>/tmp/cc.err; then echo "CCFAIL(killed-by-compile) $idx: $M"; killed=$((killed+1)); continue; fi
  goto-instrument --add-library m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --partial-loops --unwind 5 m.goto m.goto 2>/dev/null
  goto-instrument --replace-call-with-contract ZopfliSublenToCache --enforce-contract $FUNCTION m.goto mc.goto 2>/dev/null
  out=$(cbmc mc.goto --function $FUNCTION --depth 200 2>&1 | grep -E "VERIFICATION (SUCCESSFUL|FAILED)")
  if echo "$out" | grep -q SUCCESSFUL; then
    echo "SURVIVED  $idx: $M"
  else
    echo "KILLED    $idx: $M  [$out]"
    killed=$((killed+1))
  fi
done
echo "=== Killed $killed / ${#MUT[@]} ==="

2739	static void StoreInLongestMatchCache(ZopfliBlockState *s,
2740	                                     size_t pos, size_t limit,
2741	                                     const unsigned short *sublen,
2742	                                     unsigned short distance, unsigned short length)
2743	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))
2744	__CPROVER_requires(s->blockstart <= pos)
2745	__CPROVER_requires(pos - s->blockstart <= 1024)
2746	__CPROVER_requires(length <= 1024)
2747	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc, sizeof(*s->lmc)))
2748	/* The cache arrays hang two pointer levels below the parameter (s->lmc->...);
2749	   they are sized to exactly the touched slot so that the lmcpos =
2750	   pos - blockstart index computation is pinned: a mutation to pos + blockstart
2751	   reads out of bounds whenever blockstart > 0. */
2752	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(
2753	    s->lmc->length, (pos - s->blockstart + 1) * sizeof(*s->lmc->length)))
2754	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(
2755	    s->lmc->dist, (pos - s->blockstart + 1) * sizeof(*s->lmc->dist)))
2756	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(
2757	    s->lmc->sublen,
2758	    (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * (pos - s->blockstart) * 3
2759	     + (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH - 1) * 3 + 3) * sizeof(*s->lmc->sublen)))
2760	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(sublen, (length + 2) * sizeof(*sublen)))
2761	/* Unfilled cache slots carry length==1, dist==0 (see ZopfliInitCache).  A slot
2762	   is therefore either empty (length==0), in use (dist!=0), or exactly the fresh
2763	   sentinel (length==1).  This is what lets the in-body assert
2764	   length==1 && dist==0 hold on the !cache_available path. */
2765	__CPROVER_requires(s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart] == 0
2766	                   || s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart] != 0
2767	                   || s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart] == 1)
2768	__CPROVER_assigns(
2769	    s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart],
2770	    s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart],
2771	    __CPROVER_object_from(
2772	        &s->lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * (pos - s->blockstart) * 3]))
2773	/* The slot is written iff limit is maximal and the slot was the unfilled
2774	   sentinel (old length != 0 and old dist == 0).  The exact written values pin
2775	   both ternary guards (length < ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH); otherwise the slot is
2776	   left untouched. */
2777	__CPROVER_ensures(
2778	    (limit == ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH
2779	     && __CPROVER_old(s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart]) != 0
2780	     && __CPROVER_old(s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart]) == 0)
2781	        ? (s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart]
2782	               == (length < ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH ? 0 : distance)
2783	           && s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart]
2784	               == (length < ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH ? 0 : length))
2785	        : (s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart]
2786	               == __CPROVER_old(s->lmc->dist[pos - s->blockstart])
2787	           && s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart]
2788	               == __CPROVER_old(s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart])))

2795	       that this cache value is not filled in yet. */

2810	                         size_t pos, size_t length,
2811	                         unsigned short *sublen)
2812	/* The cache window starts at sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3] and is read
2813	   at byte offsets cache[j*3 .. j*3+2] for j in [0, ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH), i.e.
2814	   the 24-byte window cache[0 .. ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH*3 - 1].  The output sublen
2815	   array is written at indices [0, cache[j*3] + 3]; the largest cached length is
2816	   255 + 3 = 258, so 259 shorts suffice.  Bound pos so the windows are finite. */
2817	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(lmc, sizeof(*lmc)))
2818	__CPROVER_requires(pos <= 1024)
2819	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(
2820	    lmc->sublen,
2821	    (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 + ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3) * sizeof(*lmc->sublen)))
2822	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(sublen, 259 * sizeof(*sublen)))
2823	__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(sublen))
2824	/* When length >= ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH the first cache entry always fills sublen[0]
2825	   with the first cached distance (the inner loop runs at least for i == 0).
2826	   Pinning that exact value kills mutations of the distance expression and of
2827	   the length < 3 guard (which would skip the write for length == 3). */
2828	__CPROVER_ensures(length < 3 ||
2829	    sublen[0] == (unsigned short)(lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 + 1]
2830	        + 256 * lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3 + 2]))
2831	{
2832	    size_t i, j;
2833	    unsigned maxlength = ZopfliMaxCachedSublen(lmc, pos, length);
2834	    unsigned prevlength = 0;
2835	    unsigned char *cache;
2836	    if (length < 3)
2837	        return;
2838	    cache = &lmc->sublen[ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * pos * 3];
2839	    for (j = 0; j < ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH; j++)
2840	    {
2841	        unsigned length = cache[j * 3] + 3;
2842	        unsigned dist = cache[j * 3 + 1] + 256 * cache[j * 3 + 2];
2843	        for (i = prevlength; i <= length; i++)
2844	        {
2845	            sublen[i] = dist;
2846	        }
2847	        if (length == maxlength)
2848	            break;
2849	        prevlength = length + 1;
2850	    }
2851	}

# Memory Predicates

## Table of Contents

* [The \_\_CPROVER\_pointer\_equals predicate](#the-__cprover_pointer_equals-predicate)
* [The \_\_CPROVER\_is\_fresh predicate](#the-__cprover_is_fresh-predicate)
* [The \_\_CPROVER\_pointer\_in\_range\_dfcc predicate](#the-__cprover_pointer_in_range_dfcc-predicate)

  * [Syntax](#syntax)
* [Using memory predicates in disjunctions](#using-memory-predicates-in-disjunctions)
* [Writing your own memory predicates](#writing-your-own-memory-predicates)

  * [Limitations](#limitations)

---

The built-in predicates discussed in this section are used to describe pointer properties in *requires clauses* and *ensures clauses*.

At a basic level, the predicates allow you to specify:

* Pointers to fresh objects (`__CPROVER_is_fresh(p, size)`),
* Aliased pointers (`__CPROVER_pointer_equals(p, q)`),
* Pointer ranges (`__CPROVER_pointer_in_range_dfcc(lb, p, ub)`).

They must be used in `requires` or `ensures` clauses—using them outside results in a verification error.

These predicates can be composed using conjunctions, implications, or disjunctions, but a `requires` or `ensures` clause must not assert multiple predicates on the same pointer simultaneously.

You can also define custom predicates to describe structures like lists, buffers, etc.

---

## The `__CPROVER_pointer_equals` predicate

This predicate checks for pointer validity and equality.

```c
bool __CPROVER_pointer_equals(void *p, void *q);
```

It returns:

* `true` if and only if:

  * `p` is either `NULL` or valid,
  * `q` is either `NULL` or valid,
  * `p == q`.

### Contract checking behavior

#### `--enforce-contract`

* In a `requires` clause: checks `q` is valid or `NULL`, then assigns `p = q`.
* In an `ensures` clause: checks `p` and `q` are valid or `NULL`, and `p == q`.

#### `--replace-call-with-contract`

* In a `requires` clause: checks both `p` and `q` are valid or `NULL`, and `p == q`.
* In an `ensures` clause: checks `q` is valid or `NULL`, then assigns `p = q`.

---

## The `__CPROVER_is_fresh` predicate

This checks pointer validity and separation.

```c
bool __CPROVER_is_fresh(void *p, size_t size);
```

Holds if:

* `p` points to a valid object with at least `size` bytes,
* The object is distinct from other objects referenced via `__CPROVER_is_fresh`.

### Contract checking behavior

#### `--enforce-contract`

* In `requires`: acts like `p = malloc(size)` nondeterministically.
* In `ensures`: checks validity and separation from all others.

#### `--replace-call-with-contract`

* In `requires`: checks pointer validity and separation.
* In `ensures`: acts like `p = malloc(size)`.

---

## The `__CPROVER_pointer_in_range_dfcc` predicate

### Syntax

```c
bool __CPROVER_pointer_in_range_dfcc(void *lb, void *p, void *ub);
```

Holds if:

* `lb`, `p`, and `ub` are valid and in the same object,
* `lb <= p && p <= ub`.

### Contract checking behavior

#### `--enforce-contract`

* In `requires`: checks `lb`, `ub` valid and assigns `p` nondeterministically between them.
* In `ensures`: checks validity and that `p` is within bounds.

#### `--replace-call-with-contract`

* In `requires`: checks validity and bounds.
* In `ensures`: assigns `p` nondeterministically within bounds.

---

void ZopfliFindLongestMatch(ZopfliBlockState *s, const ZopfliHash *h,
                            const unsigned char *array,
                            size_t pos, size_t size, size_t limit,
                            unsigned short *sublen, unsigned short *distance, unsigned short *length)
/* Block state, its longest-match cache and the cache arrays must be readable;
   they are sized exactly as the two cache callees (TryGetFromLongestMatchCache,
   StoreInLongestMatchCache) require so their preconditions are met at the call
   sites.  pos sits at or after the block start and the in-block offset is
   bounded so the cache windows are finite. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s, sizeof(*s)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(s->lmc, sizeof(*s->lmc)))
__CPROVER_requires(s->blockstart <= pos)
__CPROVER_requires(pos - s->blockstart <= 1024)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(
    s->lmc->length, (pos - s->blockstart + 1) * sizeof(*s->lmc->length)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(
    s->lmc->dist, (pos - s->blockstart + 1) * sizeof(*s->lmc->dist)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(
    s->lmc->sublen,
    (ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * (pos - s->blockstart) * 3 + ZOPFLI_CACHE_LENGTH * 3)
        * sizeof(*s->lmc->sublen)))
/* The cached slot is a valid combination (empty / in-use / fresh sentinel) and
   its length is a real, in-data match length, so the early cache-hit return
   preserves pos + *length <= size. */

                   || s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart] == 1)

__CPROVER_requires(s->lmc->length[pos - s->blockstart] <= size - pos)

__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, size * sizeof(*array)))
__CPROVER_requires(pos >= 8)

__CPROVER_requires(size <= 1024)

#!/bin/bash
# Kill-test harness for ZopfliFindLongestMatch.
# Parses avocado mutants (unified diffs), applies each by line number, runs the
# enforce-contract pipeline, and reports SURVIVED/KILLED.
set -u
SRC=/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c
FUNCTION=ZopfliFindLongestMatch
WORK=/app/_kill_zflm
DEPTH=${DEPTH:-200}
rm -rf $WORK; mkdir -p $WORK; cd $WORK

get-mutants --function $FUNCTION --file $SRC > mutants.txt 2>/dev/null

python3 - "$SRC" "$DEPTH" "$FUNCTION" <<'PY'
import re, subprocess, sys, shutil, os
SRC, DEPTH, FUNCTION = sys.argv[1], sys.argv[2], sys.argv[3]
text = open("mutants.txt").read()
blocks = text.split("--- original")[1:]
muts = []
for b in blocks:
    m = re.search(r'@@ -(\d+) \+\d+ @@', b)
    if not m: continue
    line = int(m.group(1))
    lines = b.splitlines()
    orig = mut = None
    for l in lines:
        if l.startswith('-    ') or (l.startswith('-') and not l.startswith('---')):
            if orig is None: orig = l[1:]
        elif l.startswith('+    ') or (l.startswith('+') and not l.startswith('+++')):
            if mut is None: mut = l[1:]
    # robust: collect first '-' and first '+' content lines
    orig = mut = None
    for l in lines:
        if l.startswith('-') and not l.startswith('---') and orig is None:
            orig = l[1:]
        if l.startswith('+') and not l.startswith('+++') and mut is None:
            mut = l[1:]
    if orig is not None and mut is not None:
        muts.append((line, orig, mut))

base = open(SRC).read().splitlines(keepends=True)
killed = survived = err = 0
surv_list = []
for i,(line,orig,mut) in enumerate(muts):
    lines = base[:]
    idx = line-1
    if lines[idx].rstrip('\n') != orig.rstrip('\n'):
        # try to find by content near line
        print(f"SKIP {i} line{line}: mismatch\n   file:{lines[idx].rstrip()}\n   orig:{orig.rstrip()}")
        err+=1; continue
    nl = '\n' if lines[idx].endswith('\n') else ''
    lines[idx] = mut.rstrip('\n')+nl
    open("mutated.c","w").writelines(lines)
    for f in ("m.goto","mc.goto"):
        if os.path.exists(f): os.remove(f)
    cc = subprocess.run(["goto-cc","-D__NO_CTYPE","-o","m.goto","mutated.c","-I","/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code","--function",FUNCTION], capture_output=True)
    if cc.returncode!=0:
        print(f"KILLED(compile) {i} line{line}: {mut.strip()}"); killed+=1; continue
    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument","--add-library","m.goto","m.goto"], capture_output=True)
    subprocess.run(["goto-instrument","--partial-loops","--unwind","5","m.goto","m.goto"], capture_output=True)
    inst = subprocess.run(["goto-instrument",
        "--replace-call-with-contract","TryGetFromLongestMatchCache",
        "--replace-call-with-contract","GetMatch",
        "--replace-call-with-contract","StoreInLongestMatchCache",
        "--enforce-contract",FUNCTION,"m.goto","mc.goto"], capture_output=True)
    if inst.returncode!=0:
        print(f"KILLED(instrument) {i} line{line}: {mut.strip()}"); killed+=1; continue
    cb = subprocess.run(["cbmc","mc.goto","--function",FUNCTION,"--depth",DEPTH], capture_output=True, text=True)
    out = cb.stdout+cb.stderr
    if "VERIFICATION SUCCESSFUL" in out:
        print(f"SURVIVED {i} line{line}: {mut.strip()}"); survived+=1; surv_list.append((line,mut.strip()))
    elif "VERIFICATION FAILED" in out:
        print(f"KILLED {i} line{line}: {mut.strip()}"); killed+=1
    else:
        print(f"ERR {i} line{line}: {mut.strip()}\n{out[-300:]}"); err+=1
print(f"\n=== KILLED {killed} / {len(muts)}  (survived {survived}, err {err}) ===")
print("Survivors by line:")
from collections import Counter
c=Counter(l for l,_ in surv_list)
for l,n in sorted(c.items()): print(f"  line {l}: {n}")
PY
